name: Wave Confinement Theory
description: Machine-readable, provenance-preserving cross-repository manifest for the Wave Confinement Theory research corpus.

status_policy.PASS: The assigned executable check succeeds under its declared assumptions.
status_policy.CONDITIONAL: Additional mathematical, domain, regularity, model, or empirical assumptions remain required.
status_policy.DEFINITION: The object is a definition, ansatz, or bookkeeping object rather than a theorem.
status_policy.OPEN: Analysis, formal proof, calibrated simulation, or experiment remains unresolved.
status_policy.FAIL: The encoded statement is contradicted by its assigned checker.
status_policy.PROVED: A named declaration is accepted by the Lean kernel under the hypotheses in its theorem statement.

audit_summary.source_of_truth: compiled-registry.json generated from the baseline registry, effective overrides, verification metadata, assumption registry, canonical equations, and Lean map

assumptions.name: Compatible loop orientation
assumptions.statement: The winding orientation and the curvature orientation use the same sign convention.

assumptions.name: Exact integrated phase-curvature locking
assumptions.statement: The integrated phase winding equals the integrated curvature contribution without a residual mismatch term.

assumptions.name: Constant positive loop weight
assumptions.statement: The loop weight is positive and constant, so weighted and unweighted loop averages coincide.

assumptions.name: Positive spectral offset
assumptions.statement: The Green-kernel denominator contains a strictly positive offset, preventing a pole on the declared domain.

assumptions.name: Standard Sobolev domain hypotheses
assumptions.statement: The spatial domain satisfies the regularity and extension hypotheses required by the stated Sobolev embedding.

assumptions.name: H2 confinement route
assumptions.statement: The declared confinement and stability argument requires control through the H2-to-L-infinity regularity route.

claims.title: Curvature-derived mass
unresolved_obligations: Establish that the dynamically selected curvature scale determines physical mass rather than merely supplying a dimensionally valid mapping.
Close or calibrate the model-dependent proportionality in E19.

claims.title: Finite-band pattern selection
unresolved_obligations: Distinguish linear spectral selection from nonlinear global pattern selection.
Connect the selected shell to calibrated experimental observables.

claims.title: Spatial-dimensionality stability criterion
unresolved_obligations: Prove that the H2 regularity route is necessary for every admissible stable WCT confinement mechanism, or retain explicitly conditional wording.

claims.title: Koide mass relation from curvature-harmonic structure
unresolved_obligations: Determine whether Q = 2/3 is an output or an imposed constraint.
Record which model choices are fixed before evaluating the ratio.
Count available continuous and discrete choices.
Determine whether the selected harmonic state is unique.
Determine dependence on normalization and state selection.
Classify the result as a scale-free phenomenological derivation, fitted relation, or dynamical prediction.

claims.title: Curvature-bounded computation
unresolved_obligations: Specify the encoding, precision model, update cost, and finite curvature-resource bound.
Separate physical-resource claims from classical complexity-class equivalence claims.

objects.name: Curvature-locking functional
objects.formula:
\sigma(s):=\sqrt{\kappa(s)^2+\tau(s)^2}, \qquad \langle f\rangle_w:= \frac{\oint_\Gamma w f\,ds}{\oint_\Gamma w\,ds}, \quad \oint_\Gamma w\,ds>0.

S_{\rm lock}[\varphi] = \oint_\Gamma w(s)\bigl(\partial_s\varphi-\sigma\bigr)^2\,ds.

m=\frac{\hbar}{c}\langle\sigma\rangle_w.
objects.definition: A loop-locking variational functional whose stationary configurations relate phase winding to averaged curve curvature. The mass interpretation requires the explicit locking, orientation, and weighting assumptions recorded for E5.

verification.meaning: The assigned executable check is reported under its declared assumptions.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Nonsingular curvature operator and Lyapunov candidate
objects.definition: The complex-safe regularized reciprocal and curvature-feedback operator. Positivity of the modulus-squared denominator removes the historical scalar zero; this does not by itself prove global PDE stability or uniqueness.

verification.meaning: The assigned executable check is reported under its declared assumptions.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Finite-band spectral selector
objects.definition: A Swift–Hohenberg-type finite-band selector whose Fourier symbol damps modes away from the preferred shell. It establishes the linear spectral rail, not global nonlinear pattern selection.

verification.meaning: The assigned executable check is reported under its declared assumptions.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Dimensional stability threshold
objects.definition: The standard Sobolev threshold H²→L∞ for integer spatial dimension n≤3. This is a regularity threshold and not, by itself, a universal theorem that all stable confinement is impossible above three dimensions.

verification.meaning: The standard Sobolev embedding threshold H2 to L-infinity holds under the declared domain hypotheses for integer spatial dimension n less than or equal to 3.
limitations: This standard embedding theorem is not by itself a complete nonlinear WCT stability theorem.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Curvature-bounded computation
objects.definition: A local discrete update architecture proposed for curvature-bounded computation. Complexity conclusions additionally require a fixed encoding, precision model, update cost, and finite physical-resource bound.

verification.meaning: The assigned executable check is reported under its declared assumptions.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Weighted loop average
objects.family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
objects.definition: The normalized weighted loop average for a nonnegative weight with nonzero total weight. It preserves the dimension of the averaged quantity but does not select a physical weighting measure.

verification.meaning: The positive weighted-average construction has a nonzero denominator and preserves the encoded dimension of the averaged quantity.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Loop-locking action
objects.family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
objects.formula: S_{\rm eff}[\varphi] = \oint_\Gamma w(\partial_s\varphi-\sigma)^2\,ds + \Lambda \left( \oint_\Gamma\partial_s\varphi\,ds-2\pi n \right), \qquad n\in\mathbb Z.
objects.definition: A constrained phase-curvature mismatch action. Nonnegative weighting makes its squared mismatch term nonnegative; existence and uniqueness of continuum minimizers require separate analysis.

verification.meaning: The assigned executable check is reported under its declared assumptions.

limitations: Finite algebraic support; this is not an integral theorem on general curves.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Effective-wavenumber chain
objects.family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
objects.formula:
L_s:=\oint_\Gamma ds, \qquad k_{\rm wind}:=\frac{2\pi|n|}{L_s}, \qquad k_\sigma:=\frac1{L_s}\oint_\Gamma\sigma\,ds.

k_{\rm wind}=k_\sigma=\langle\sigma\rangle_w
objects.definition: The effective-wavenumber identification connecting phase winding, integrated curvature, and the weighted curvature average. The chain is derived only under compatible orientation, exact integrated locking, and a positive constant-weight condition.

verification.meaning: Exact loop closure and the declared orientation and weighting assumptions establish the encoded effective-wavenumber chain.
limitations: This does not independently establish that physical particle masses are generated by the model.
Does not prove dynamic attainment of exact lock.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Mass-curvature law
objects.family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
objects.formula:
E_{\rm rest}=\hbar c\,k_{\rm eff}, \qquad m=\frac{\hbar}{c}k_{\rm eff}.

m=\frac{\hbar}{c}\langle\sigma\rangle_w.
objects.definition: A dimensionally consistent mapping from an effective inverse-length scale to rest energy and mass. The PASS establishes dimensional closure, not that WCT dynamics generates observed particle masses.

verification.meaning: The encoded mass-curvature relation has the dimensions of mass.
limitations: Dimensional closure does not establish the physical identification of mass with curvature.
Dimensions only; not the physical identification.

source_papers.title: Rest Energy from Density-Weighted Loop Curvature: A Covariant Locking Principle

notes: E5 remains conditional unless exact locking and compatible weighting assumptions are supplied.

objects.name: Solenoidal mass law
objects.family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
objects.definition: The solenoidal form of the mass-curvature mapping using an averaged curve-curvature magnitude. Its physical prediction requires a specified averaging measure and a dynamically selected geometry.

verification.meaning: The solenoidal curvature average has the dimensions required by the stated mass relation.
limitations: The averaging measure and physical identification require separate justification.
Dimensions only.

notes: Symbolic status, verification kind, Lean coverage, and empirical state are generated from the compiled registry.

objects.name: Dimensional stability criterion
objects.family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
objects.definition: The conditional WCT dimensional-stability criterion combining the Sobolev threshold with additional confinement hypotheses. It is not a proved biconditional characterization of all stable WCT solutions.

verification.meaning: The Sobolev threshold supports the declared three-dimensional regularity route; the universal WCT stability conclusion remains conditional.
limitations: This is not a biconditional characterization of every stable WCT solution.
Failure of the H2 embedding route above three dimensions is not by itself a universal impossibility theorem.

source_papers.title: Hard Upper Bound on Spatial Dimensionality in Wave Confinement Theory

notes: The n <= 3 embedding threshold is not represented as a proof that all WCT equilibria are stable in three dimensions or unstable in higher dimensions.

machine_reading_guidance.canonical_id_policy: Use canonical_id as the primary key. Aliases must not be silently collapsed.
machine_reading_guidance.verification_rule: Read verification.outcome together with verification.kind and verification.scope. PASS alone does not state what kind of check succeeded.
machine_reading_guidance.formalization_rule: Never report a SymPy PASS as a Lean proof. Only named kernel-accepted declarations may be labeled PROVED.
machine_reading_guidance.empirical_rule: Empirical validation is independent of symbolic and formal status.
machine_reading_guidance.missing_data_rule: Use null, empty arrays, UNKNOWN, or OPEN rather than inventing identifiers, proofs, dates, dependencies, or evidence.
